evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useAutoTopup.ts` encapsulates billing/auth edge-function behavior (`setup-auto-topup`, `creditQueryKeys`) but is placed under a generic shared hook bucket.

`src/shared/hooks/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts` owns project/video thumbnail pipeline behavior and query invalidation for generation data, also under shared hooks rather than a generation/media domain area.

`src/shared/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` and `src/shared/components/AspectRatioSelector.tsx` are domain-specific generation controls but live in `shared/components`, flattening ownership boundaries.
`src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.tsx` is app-shell infrastructure (global boundary/reload UI) but is grouped with reusable presentational components.
`tailwind.config.ts` content paths include `./pages`, `./components`, `./app`, and `./src`, indicating overlapping historical directory contracts instead of one clear top-level package shape.

Two major prop-boundary resolvers flatten large grouped contracts with spread-merges (`...displayOptions`, `...imageState`, `...editActions`, `...shotWorkflow`; similarly for join-clips form), creating implicit precedence and collision risk at integration seams.

Task/orchestration boundary contracts are weakened at handoff points (`buildOrchestrationContract` returns `Record<string, unknown>` and video generation forces `requestBody as unknown as TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams`), so invalid payload drift can cross module boundaries undetected.

Edge shared helpers use mixed boundary failure semantics: some paths return `EdgeOperationResult` (`billing.ts`, thumbnail handling in storage), while sibling paths throw raw errors (`handleStorageOperations` upload failure) or return permissive fail-open structs (`rateLimit.ts`).

Invalidation orchestration has non-uniform lifecycle semantics: delayed invalidation in `useInvalidateGenerations` is fire-and-forget via timers without hook-level cleanup, while other queue flows (timeline write queue) explicitly serialize and expose lifecycle events.

Generation-to-UI transformation seams are inconsistent by consumer path: `transformGeneration` derives thumbnail via fallback logic, while `transformForTimeline` hardcodes `thumbUrl` to main location and bypasses thumbnail normalization.
`useButtonGroupProps` builds four near-identical prop objects via thin helper functions and memoizes on `[params]`, so object recreation and indirection remain while local logic stays minimal.

`useImageEditValue` and `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` contain large manual property-mapping blocks that restate structure rather than compute behavior, making local edits high-friction and error-prone.

`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` introduces layered result wrappers/adapters (`runCallback` + `*Result` + `*Adapter`) that multiply local branches and placeholder return paths for simple callback forwarding.

Migration residue remains in primary type surfaces: `LegacyProjectImageSettings` with multiple `@deprecated` fields and a dated sunset contract in `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts` lines 101-128.

Legacy decode fields are still carried in active config types (`StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata` keeps `motion_strength`, `structure_type`, `uni3c_*`) in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts` lines 123-132.
A deprecated compatibility alias is still exported (`getStoragePublicUrl = buildStoragePublicUrl`) in `supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts` lines 216-219, extending dual-path API usage.

Client-side flows mix strategies in the same feature: `useLoraSubmission` emits `toast.error(...)` and returns `null` (`src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/MyLorasTab/hooks/useLoraSubmission.ts:160-163`, `:180-183`, `:385-388`) while nearby paths use centralized `handleError(...)` (`:445-449`).

Background/helper code sometimes swallows failures with no propagation or telemetry (`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx:10-12`, `:42-44`, `:169-170`, `:177-178`; `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts:10-14`) unlike other modules that normalize/log with `handleError`.

Edge/server error surfaces are inconsistent: `calculate-task-cost` returns raw caught message in HTTP response (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:494-497`), while `complete_task/generation` logs then rethrows (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:143-149`) and other paths return `null` sentinels (`:58-67`).

findings.summary: Shared error handler also owns UI notification bus wiring, coupling domain error flow to browser runtime concerns.
evidence: `handleError.ts` defines `ERROR_NOTIFICATION_EVENT`, mutable module-level notifier state, and window listener registration in `configureErrorNotifier` in addition to error normalization/logging.
Toast infrastructure (`sonner.tsx`) is a separate UI subsystem, but notification dispatch contract is embedded in shared error core rather than an app-layer adapter.
findings.suggestion: Extract browser notification transport into an app/UI boundary module (for example `errorNotifierBridge`) and keep `handleError` focused on normalization/logging plus callback hooks; inject notifier behavior from composition root.

findings.summary: A single shared hook module owns multiple device policies, creating a broad change hotspot with unclear responsibility boundaries.
evidence: `useMobile.tsx` includes UA heuristics, pointer detection, orientation listeners, layout column derivation, and both hook and non-hook APIs (`isMobileUA`).
Feature components (`CreativePartnerPane`, `InfoTooltip`) consume this global policy surface, so policy changes ripple across unrelated Home and gallery behaviors.
findings.suggestion: Split device capability concerns into focused modules (viewport breakpoints, touch capability, orientation/layout policy), then expose a small composed API for consumers that truly need aggregation.

findings.summary: Generation domain type index re-exports from generic root types, obscuring true ownership of domain contracts.
evidence: `src/domains/generation/types/index.ts` exports symbols from `@/types/generation*` and `@/types/shot` rather than domain-local definitions.
Gallery hooks import `Shot` via the domain path, but the contracts are actually anchored elsewhere, so the domain layer acts as a pass-through façade.
findings.suggestion: Move generation contracts under `src/domains/generation/types/*` as canonical ownership, then adapt root-level aliases (if needed) to point inward, not vice versa.

findings.summary: Install-instructions behavior depends on string-key synchronization across scenario resolution and visual registry, increasing ripple edits.
evidence: `resolveInstallVisualKey` returns `InstallVisualKey` literals that must match keys in `installVisualRegistry` exactly.
`visuals.tsx` is a large monolithic registry file containing all platform renderers, so adding or changing one scenario requires touching central registry and selector logic together.
findings.suggestion: Define scenario objects as typed records containing both predicate and renderer reference (or lazy import), so mapping is declarative and localized per platform/scenario instead of cross-file string contracts.

findings.summary: Task boundary contracts are fragmented into multiple oversized param-bag builders with duplicated translation and validation logic.
evidence: Each module independently validates required fields and builds large `Record<string, unknown>` payloads with similar defaulting/normalization patterns.
Field-shaping behavior is distributed (e.g., expanded arrays, conditional top-level keys, orchestration contract insertion), so cross-task changes require touching several implementations.
The same conceptual seam (UI/task params -> worker/orchestrator payload) is not centralized, which increases integration inconsistency risk.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared typed task-payload pipeline (normalize -> validate -> serialize) with task-specific extensions; keep one canonical boundary schema per task family and reuse common validators/default resolvers from a single module.

findings.summary: Import-time Supabase singletons create implicit dependency coupling across hooks/services.
evidence: Multiple modules define `const supabase = getSupabaseClient()` at module scope rather than at call-site or via injected dependency.
This hides lifecycle requirements in import order and makes seam-level replacement harder for tests and alternative runtimes.
The pattern is repeated across unrelated feature boundaries, so dependency behavior is globally coupled rather than explicit per operation.
findings.suggestion: Use a small dependency-provider abstraction (`getDb()` or injected client param) at function/hook boundary; default to shared client but allow explicit override for tests and non-browser contexts.

findings.summary: Local implementation relies on high-volume pass-through mapping layers that add indirection without adding behavior.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` has four builder helpers that mostly repeat shared fields and forward inputs unchanged.
`useImageEditValue` is dominated by large state-reassembly helpers that mirror source models rather than transform logic.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` composes many memoized object maps with long dependency arrays, making local edits verbose and easy to desynchronize.
findings.suggestion: Collapse pass-through layers: move shared fields into a single base object, use narrower typed slices per consumer, and keep only mappings that actually transform semantics.

findings.summary: Callback handling in shot-image editor is over-wrapped into result/adapter tiers, increasing branch noise for simple flows.
evidence: `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` layers `runCallback`, specialized `*Result` wrappers, and final `*Adapter` wrappers before reaching actual operations.
Several error paths return placeholder values (`false`, empty IDs) rather than preserving one clear failure channel.
The callback surface becomes harder to reason about because control flow is split across wrapper strata.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one callback contract (throwing or typed result) and remove adapter tiers; keep a single wrapper for logging/toast policy and let callers handle a uniform failure shape.

findings.summary: Cross-module prop contracts are flattened with spread-merges, making boundary precedence implicit and fragile.
evidence: `resolveShotImagesEditorProps` merges four large prop groups by spread order (lines 174-180), so duplicate keys silently override earlier groups.
`resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps` repeats the same pattern (lines 106-111), creating another implicit override seam between clip settings, motion config, and UI state.
findings.suggestion: Replace flattening with explicit nested props in consumers, or enforce a compile-time collision guard (e.g., disjoint key utility type + explicit mapping) so boundary ownership is unambiguous.

findings.summary: Payload handoffs across task-generation boundaries lose type guarantees through generic records and double-casts.
evidence: `buildOrchestrationContract` returns `Record<string, unknown>` (line 17) rather than a concrete contract type, weakening downstream guarantees.
`generateVideo` builds a broad `Record<string, unknown>` request and submits via `requestBody as unknown as TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` (line 701), bypassing structural validation at the seam.
findings.suggestion: Define strict DTO types for orchestration contract and travel request payloads, return those from builders, and remove `unknown` bridge casts by validating and narrowing before submission.

findings.summary: Shared edge modules expose inconsistent failure contracts (result object vs throw vs fail-open), making caller behavior unpredictable.
evidence: `billing.ts` returns `EdgeOperationResult` for cost-trigger failures (`edgeFailure`/`edgeSuccess`), while `storage.ts` throws on upload failure (`throw new Error(...)`, line 77) but uses `EdgeOperationResult` for thumbnail outcomes.
`rateLimit.ts` defines a separate `RateLimitResult` with policy/errorCode fail-open semantics, parallel to but disconnected from `EdgeOperationResult` conventions.
findings.suggestion: Standardize edge boundary outcomes on one shared result contract (or one shared throw strategy) and provide adapters at outer handlers so integration points can compose predictable lifecycle/error behavior.

findings.summary: Generation invalidation scheduling exposes mixed completion semantics and timer lifecycle handling at integration boundaries.
evidence: `useInvalidateGenerations` returns `Promise<void>` but delayed mode returns immediately after scheduling timeout (lines 81-89), so callers cannot await actual invalidation completion.
Timeout map entries are only cleared on same-shot reschedule or callback execution; no hook unmount cleanup path is present.
By contrast, timeline write orchestration explicitly models queued/start/end phases and serialized completion, highlighting inconsistency in lifecycle contract discipline.
findings.suggestion: Expose a consistent async contract for scheduled invalidations (e.g., return cancellable handles/promises), add unmount cleanup, and align lifecycle semantics with other orchestration utilities.

findings.summary: Sibling generation transformation paths produce divergent thumbnail and metadata semantics, increasing glue logic at call sites.
evidence: `transformGeneration` uses layered thumbnail extraction/fallback and URL normalization (`extractThumbnailUrl`, identity stripping), while `transformForTimeline` sets `thumbUrl` directly to `genData.location` (line 405), ignoring `thumbnail_url`.
`useLineageChain` exposes a separate `thumbnailUrl` chain model from raw variant data, introducing a third representation path for similar UI needs.
findings.suggestion: Centralize a single generation/variant view-model adapter for thumbnail/url fields and reuse it across timeline, lineage, and gallery seams to eliminate per-consumer translation drift.

findings.summary: Aspect-ratio fallback behavior is implemented in two separate helpers with overlapping responsibility.
evidence: `getAspectRatioStyle` returns parsed project ratio or `'1'` default in `image-utils.ts` lines 58-66.
`getImageAspectRatioStyle` independently repeats the same project-ratio parsing and `'1'` fallback in `imageAspectRatio.ts` lines 61-68.
findings.suggestion: Extract one shared `resolveAspectRatioStyle` helper and route both call sites through it so defaults and parsing rules cannot diverge.

findings.summary: Text-case toggle uses closure state inversion instead of functional update, making the state transition less robust.
evidence: `useTextCase` defines `toggle` as `setPreserveUserText(!preserveUserText)` in `useTextCase.ts` line 25.
Nearby hooks in the same layer already depend on stable callback semantics (`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` lines 31-46), so this closure-style inversion is a weaker local pattern.
findings.suggestion: Use functional updates (`setPreserveUserText(prev => !prev)`) to make the toggle atomic and remove closure sensitivity.

findings.summary: Migration-era legacy fields remain embedded in canonical public types, prolonging dual-shape maintenance.
evidence: `LegacyProjectImageSettings` is still part of accepted input via `ProjectImageSettingsInput` in `ImageGenerationForm/types.ts` lines 109-136.
`StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata` still carries legacy guidance fields for decode compatibility in `travelBetweenImages/types.ts` lines 123-132.
findings.suggestion: Move legacy decode contracts to dedicated migration adapters/modules and keep exported runtime-facing types canonical-only.

findings.summary: Deprecated storage URL alias remains exported, which encourages continued usage of the old path.
evidence: `getStoragePublicUrl` is explicitly deprecated but still exported as alias to `buildStoragePublicUrl` in `storage.ts` lines 216-219.
The codebase already uses explicit sunset contracts elsewhere (`ImageGenerationForm/types.ts` lines 104-108), but this alias has no comparable removal gate.
findings.suggestion: Add a hard deprecation window (or removal ticket/date) and migrate call sites to `buildStoragePublicUrl`, then delete the alias.

findings.summary: Domain-specific generation UI is stored in `shared/components`, obscuring ownership and expected change boundaries.
evidence: `ActiveLoRAsDisplay` depends on LoRA-specific models/types (`LoraDisplayModel`, `ActiveLora`, trigger words, LoRA strength).
`AspectRatioSelector` depends on generation-specific ratio mappings (`ASPECT_RATIO_TO_RESOLUTION`) and visualizer behavior, not a generic cross-domain primitive.
findings.suggestion: Move these components into a generation-focused package (for example `src/domains/generation/components/...`) and keep `shared/components` for truly domain-agnostic primitives.

findings.summary: Feature workflows with strong business context are placed in `shared/hooks`, creating a catch-all layer instead of domain-owned modules.
evidence: `useAutoTopup` orchestrates auth session retrieval, users table fields, and a billing edge function (`setup-auto-topup`).
`useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator` manages generation/video queueing, thumbnail pipeline, and generation query cache updates.
`useBatchImageLoading` is labeled for batch view components and couples to app settings/media URL policy; it is feature workflow glue rather than a broadly reusable hook.
findings.suggestion: Relocate hooks to domain/feature folders (billing/credits, media-generation, gallery/batch) and expose only stable shared abstractions from `shared/hooks`.

findings.summary: Global app-shell error boundary is packaged as a shared component, blurring framework boundary lines.
evidence: `AppErrorBoundary` is explicitly app-level infrastructure (global fallback UI and page reload/retry behavior), not a reusable feature component.
Root-level infrastructure concerns are already centralized (e.g., Vite app config), but app-shell runtime boundary code is mixed into `shared/components`.
findings.suggestion: Move `AppErrorBoundary` to an app-shell package (for example `src/app/boundaries`), and keep `shared/components` focused on reusable UI blocks.

findings.summary: Root config still encodes multiple historical directory conventions, weakening package-shape clarity.
evidence: `tailwind.config.ts` scans `./pages`, `./components`, `./app`, and `./src` simultaneously, signaling overlapping top-level organization models.
Current feature code lives under `src/shared/...`, but config still treats multiple roots as active first-class package boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Converge on one canonical source root and prune obsolete content globs after verifying no active files depend on legacy top-level roots.

findings.summary: Generation domain contracts are presented as domain-owned but source from global type buckets, weakening ownership boundaries.
evidence: src/domains/generation/types/index.ts re-exports GenerationParams, GenerationRow, Shot, and generation metadata from '@/types/*' instead of domain-local modules.
README.md positions architecture docs/contracts as trustworthy ownership guidance, but this entry point makes domain contract location ambiguous for future changes.
findings.suggestion: Either move generation contract types into domain-owned paths (and export from there) or explicitly document that '@/types/*' is the canonical contract layer and make domain barrels thin compatibility shims with a deprecation policy.

findings.summary: The `cn` compatibility alias introduces redundant public surface without an explicit compatibility policy.
evidence: src/shared/lib/ui/cn.ts only re-exports `cn` from '@/shared/lib/uiClassNames' and adds no behavior.
src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx imports from the alias path, so both alias and source path can become de facto contracts.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical import path for `cn` and enforce it with lint/codemod; if the alias must remain for migration, mark it as compatibility-only with a removal timeline.

findings.summary: Core error normalization and browser notification transport are coupled in one shared module, diluting architectural contract clarity.
evidence: handleError.ts includes normalization/logging plus browser event wiring (`window.dispatchEvent`, notifier registration state, listener lifecycle) in the same file.
README.md states shared modules should remain UI-agnostic unless bootstrap wiring runs, but this module still owns UI transport mechanics directly.
findings.suggestion: Split error-core behavior (normalize/log/rethrow) from UI transport wiring into separate modules, with one explicit composition point in app bootstrap so runtime contracts are enforced by module boundaries.

findings.summary: LoRA submission paths mix sentinel returns + direct toasts with centralized error normalization, producing inconsistent failure behavior.
evidence: `uploadToHuggingFaceIfNeeded` uses `toast.error(...)` and returns `null` on upload failure (`useLoraSubmission.ts:160-163`, `:180-183`), then caller exits early (`:385-388`) without normalized error context.

Same module later uses `handleError(...)` in catch (`useLoraSubmission.ts:445-449`), while sibling mutation hooks standardize on throw + `onError` with `handleError` (`useGenerationMutations.ts:49-50`, `:99-103`, `:163-165`, `:195-197`).
findings.suggestion: Use one contract per feature: throw typed/domain errors from internal async helpers and route all user-visible/logging behavior through `handleError` at mutation boundary; reserve sentinel `null` only for truly non-error optional flows.

findings.summary: Several catch blocks silently suppress runtime failures, creating hidden and non-diagnosable behavior differences.
evidence: `useMobile` catches exceptions and returns fallback values or no-ops without logging (`useMobile.tsx:10-12`, `:42-44`, `:169-170`, `:177-178`).
`useDiscordSignIn` intentionally ignores localStorage failure in inner catch (`useDiscordSignIn.ts:10-14`) while other errors in same function go through `handleError` (`:24-27`).
findings.suggestion: Keep graceful fallback behavior, but add lightweight structured diagnostics (e.g., `handleError(..., { showToast: false, context, logData })`) for suppressed exceptions so operational failures are observable and consistent.

findings.summary: Supabase edge flows expose inconsistent failure contracts across similar orchestration/cost paths.
evidence: `calculate-task-cost` catch returns `jsonResponse({ error: error?.message || 'Unknown error occurred' }, 500)` (`index.ts:494-497`), leaking raw runtime messages to clients.
`complete_task/generation` catches, logs, then rethrows (`generation.ts:143-149`), while normal control paths also use `return null` sentinels for non-generation tasks (`generation.ts:58-67`).
`orchestrator` wraps many failures with contextual `new Error(..., { cause })` (`orchestrator.ts:73-76`, `:216-218`, `:242-244`), giving a different contract again.
findings.suggestion: Define a shared edge-function error policy: consistent error envelope for HTTP responses, consistent wrapping/preservation of cause, and explicit typed result semantics for "skip" vs "failure" paths.
